s_row = np.load('s0.npy')\n# sigma_normal per m-bin: 0.19 below 2.7, ramp to 0.275 above (from clean day1-2 MLE)\ncenters = np.concatenate([[hmm.EDGES[0]-0.05], (hmm.EDGES[:-1]+hmm.EDGES[1:])/2, [hmm.EDGES[-1]+0.05]])\nsig_bins = np.clip(0.19 + (centers-2.7)*0.43, 0.19, 0.275)\ntabs = np.zeros((4, hmm.NB, hmm.KMAX+1))\nfor c in range(4):\n    sg = sig_bins if c==0 else np.full(hmm.NB, 0.19)\n    tabs[c] = hmm.qty_dist_vec(centers * hmm.TAU[c], sg)\n\nq = 0.01\nfor it in range(12):\n    E, mx, IB = hmm.emis_logE(M, s_row, tabs)\n    ll, grej, Wn, H = hmm.forward_backward(E, mx, q)\n    q_new = grej[:, :364].sum() / hmm.PUR[:, :364].sum()\n    M, s_row = hmm.fit_M(Wn)\n    print(f'iter {it}: loglik {ll:.0f} q {q:.5f}->{q_new:.5f}', flush=True)\n    if abs(q_new-q) < 1e-5 and it>3: q=q_new; break\n    q = q_new\n\nnp.save('gamma_rej2.npy', grej); np.save('M_em2.npy', M); np.save('s_em2.npy', s_row)\ng = grej[(hmm.SOLD>0)]\nprint('q final', round(q,5), 'expected rejections:', round(g.sum()))\nfor thr in [0.5, 0.8, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99, 0.999]:\n    print(f'gamma > {thr}: {(g>thr).sum()}')\nEOF","description":"Re-run HMM with fixed parametric stage emissions, learn q only","timeout":1800000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3135,"cache_read_input_tokens":181121,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3135,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":43,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"3095601a-fd94-4997-88e0-51f3aedf45a5","request_id":"req_011CbwR1hnftLufuLxcwFWfw"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01PSEYiYRoFwgtwaSSwQbTyr","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Sz5CYyQuCegsLfCvhDQYXy","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport numpy as np, pickle, collections, pandas as pd\n\ngrej = np.load('gamma_rej2.npy')\npurch = pickle.load(open('purchases.pkl','rb'))\nD = pickle.load(open('deliveries.pkl','rb'))\nbatches = D['batches']\ndf = pd.read_pickle('sales.pkl')\nkeys = pd.read_csv('panel_keys.csv').iloc[:,0].values\nkey_ix = {k:i for i,k in enumerate(keys)}\nrows = (df.Store_ID + '|' + df.cons.astype(str) + '|' + df.Product_ID).map(key_ix).values\ndays = df.Day.values; solds = df.Sold_Quantity.values; prods = df.Product_ID.values\nidxs = df.index.values\n\npair_stats = collections.defaultdict(lambda: [0.0, 0.0, 0, 0, 0])  # gamma_sum, n, cnt>.9 single, cnt>.99 single, cnt>.999 single\nfor i in range(len(df)):\n    if solds[i] == 0 or days[i] > 364: continue\n    g = grej[rows[i], days[i]-1]\n    segs = purch.get(idxs[i])\n    prs = {batches[b][0] for b,_ in segs}\n    tot = sum(qq for _,qq in segs)\n    for bid, qq in segs:\n        pr = batches[bid][0]\n        st = pair_stats[(pr, prods[i])]\n        st[0] += g*qq/tot; st[1] += qq/tot\n    if len(prs) == 1:\n        pr = next(iter(prs))\n        st = pair_stats[(pr, prods[i])]\n        if g > 0.9: st[2] += 1\n        if g > 0.99: st[3] += 1\n        if g > 0.999: st[4] += 1\n\nres = [(pr, p, *st) for (pr,p), st in pair_stats.items()]\nres.sort(key=lambda x: -(x[2]/x[3]) if False else -(x[2]/max(x[3],1)))\nrates = np.array([r[2]/r[3] for r in res])\nc90 = np.array([r[4] for r in res]); c99 = np.array([r[5] for r in res]); c999 = np.array([r[6] for r in res])\nprint('pairs:', len(res))\nprint('rate (gamma/n): p10 %.4f p50 %.4f p90 %.4f max %.4f' % tuple(np.percentile(rates,[10,50,90,100])))\nprint('count>0.9 single: p10 %.0f p50 %.0f p90 %.0f max %.0f; pairs with 0: %d' % (*np.percentile(c90,[10,50,90,100]), (c90==0).sum()))\nprint('count>0.99 single: p50 %.0f max %.0f; pairs with 0: %d' % (*np.percentile(c99,[50,100]), (c99==0).sum()))\nprint('count>0.999 single: max %.0f; pairs with 0: %d' % (np.percentile(c999,100), (c999==0).sum()))\n# rate-sorted top/bottom\nres.sort(key=lambda x: -(x[2]/x[3]))\nprint('\\nTOP 20 by rate:')\nfor r in res[:20]: print(f'{r[0]:>7} {r[1]:>5} gsum={r[2]:7.1f} n={r[3]:6.0f} rate={r[2]/r[3]:.4f} c90={r[4]} c99={r[5]} c999={r[6]}')\nprint('\\nBOTTOM 20 by rate:')\nfor r in res[-20:]: print(f'{r[0]:>7} {r[1]:>5} gsum={r[2]:7.1f} n={r[3]:6.0f} rate={r[2]/r[3]:.4f} c90={r[4]} c99={r[5]} c999={r[6]}')\nwith open('pair_stats2.pkl','wb') as f: pickle.dump(res, f)\nEOF","description":"Aggregate new posteriors per producer-product pair","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5153,"cache_read_input_tokens":184256,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5153,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":50,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"f42004b0-eb8e-4f0d-aecd-40a7609b2420","request_id":"req_011CbwRC49omajSVqPt5Dy8w"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01PNQCTW4GjXrLHfTEHSj5He","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018YCFT8chx9p8iRkBQba3Ca","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport numpy as np\nimport hmm\n\nM = np.load('M_em2.npy'); s_row = np.load('s_em2.npy')\ncenters = np.concatenate([[hmm.EDGES[0]-0.05], (hmm.EDGES[:-1]+hmm.EDGES[1:])/2, [hmm.EDGES[-1]+0.05]])\nsig_bins = np.clip(0.19 + (centers-2.7)*0.43, 0.19, 0.275)\ntabs = np.zeros((4, hmm.NB, hmm.KMAX+1))\nfor c in range(4):\n    sg = sig_bins if c==0 else np.full(hmm.NB, 0.19)\n    tabs[c] = hmm.qty_dist_vec(centers * hmm.TAU[c], sg)\nIBM = np.digitize(M, hmm.EDGES)\nrng = np.random.default_rng(123)\nN, T = hmm.N, hmm.T\n\ndef simulate(q_true):\n    \"\"\"simulate panel: shopping Bern(s), qty from tabs[class]; recovery triggered at purchases (sold>0 requires... use shopped as purchase proxy? need sold -- but in sim demand>0 => purchase if inventory; assume purchase=demand>0)\"\"\"\n    X = np.zeros((N, T), dtype=np.int16)\n    state = np.zeros(N, dtype=int)  # 0 normal else recovery offset\n    for t in range(T):\n        cls = hmm.STAGE[np.minimum(state, 14)]\n        shop = rng.random(N) < s_row\n        ib = IBM[:, t]\n        P = tabs[cls, ib]                      # N x K+1\n        u = rng.random(N)[:, None]\n        qty = (u > np.cumsum(P, axis=1)).sum(axis=1)\n        x = np.where(shop, qty, 0)\n        X[:, t] = x\n        # transitions: purchase happens if x>0 (approx; ignores stockouts)\n        state = np.where(state > 0, np.where(state >= 14, 0, state + 1), 0)\n        trig = (x > 0) & (rng.random(N) < q_true)\n        state = np.where(trig, 1, state)\n    return X\n\nfor label, q_true in [('NULL', 0.0), ('INJECT', 0.009)]:\n    X = simulate(q_true)\n    # run posterior machinery with the SAME fitted params (q prior = 0.00907)\n    hmm.A = X  # monkey-patch panel\n    hmm.PUR = X > 0\n    E, mx, IB2 = hmm.emis_logE(M, s_row, tabs)\n    ll, grej, Wn, H = hmm.forward_backward(E, mx, 0.00907)\n    g = grej[X > 0]\n    print(f'{label}: purchases {len(g)}, gamma>0.5: {(g>0.5).sum()}, >0.8: {(g>0.8).sum()}, >0.9: {(g>0.9).sum()}, >0.95: {(g>0.95).sum()}, >0.99: {(g>0.99).sum()}, >0.999: {(g>0.999).sum()}, sum {g.sum():.0f}')\n    np.save(f'sim_{label}_gamma.npy', grej); np.save(f'sim_{label}_X.npy', X)\nEOF","description":"Null and injection calibration of posterior detector","timeout":1800000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4407,"cache_read_input_tokens":189409,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4407,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":62,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"39613d82-591d-4ac0-a67f-cfab79faa93b","request_id":"req_011CbwRGJxexrMyE8pTQELxx"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01Vw3xHkz2vFjVRA1EwDjij4","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DJCpQWdAcUXkYF4Q2TvFhm","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport numpy as np, pickle, collections, pandas as pd\nfrom scipy import stats\n\ngrej = np.load('gamma_rej2.npy')\npurch = pickle.load(open('purchases.pkl','rb'))\nD = pickle.load(open('deliveries.pkl','rb'))\nbatches = D['batches']\ndf = pd.read_pickle('sales.pkl')\nkeys = pd.read_csv('panel_keys.csv').iloc[:,0].values\nkey_ix = {k:i for i,k in enumerate(keys)}\nrows = (df.Store_ID + '|' + df.cons.astype(str) + '|' + df.Product_ID).map(key_ix).values\ndays = df.Day.values; solds = df.Sold_Quantity.values; prods = df.Product_ID.values\nstores = df.Store_ID.values\nidxs = df.index.values\n\n# events: gamma>0.8, single producer\nevents = []   # (producer, product, store, day, batch_id, gamma)\nexposure = collections.defaultdict(float)  # (pr, p) -> purchases\nfor i in range(len(df)):\n    if solds[i] == 0 or days[i] > 364: continue\n    segs = purch.get(idxs[i])\n    prs = {batches[b][0] for b,_ in segs}\n    if len(prs) != 1: continue\n    pr = next(iter(prs))\n    exposure[(pr, prods[i])] += 1\n    g = grej[rows[i], days[i]-1]\n    if g > 0.8:\n        events.append((pr, prods[i], stores[i], days[i], segs[0][0], g))\nprint('single-producer events gamma>0.8:', len(events))\n\n# --- test 1: within-product producer effect (chi-square on counts vs exposure) ---\nprint('\\nwithin-product chi-square (producer effects):')\nallp = 0\nfor p in [f'P_{i}' for i in range(1,31)]:\n    cnt = collections.Counter()\n    for e in events:\n        if e[1]==p: cnt[e[0]] += 1\n    prs = [pr for (pr,pp) in exposure if pp==p]\n    obs = np.array([cnt.get(pr,0) for pr in prs], float)\n    exp = np.array([exposure[(pr,p)] for pr in prs], float)\n    exp = exp/exp.sum()*obs.sum()\n    chi2 = ((obs-exp)**2/np.maximum(exp,1e-9)).sum()\n    dfree = len(prs)-1\n    pval = 1-stats.chi2.cdf(chi2, dfree)\n    if pval < 0.05: allp += 1\n    print(f'{p:>5}: n={int(obs.sum()):4d} chi2={chi2:6.1f} df={dfree} p={pval:.3f}' + (' *' if pval<0.05 else ''))\nprint('products with producer-effect p<0.05:', allp, 'of 30')\n\n# --- test 2: batch-day clustering: events per (producer, day) across products ---\nbd = collections.Counter()\nfor pr, p, s, d, b, g in events:\n    bday = batches[b][1]\n    bd[(pr, bday, p)] = bd[(pr, bday, p)]  # noop\n# events per batch (same batch multiple rejections?)\nper_batch = collections.Counter()\nfor e in events: per_batch[e[4]] += 1\nvc = collections.Counter(per_batch.values())\nprint('\\nevents per batch distribution:', dict(sorted(vc.items())))\nnb_total = len({e[4] for e in events})\n# chance expectation: events random over batches weighted by buyers (~approx): compare via simple Poisson\nprint('batches with >=2 events:', sum(v for k,v in vc.items() if k>=2))\n\n# --- test 3: same producer-day across different products (production-day effect) ---\npd_cnt = collections.Counter()\nfor pr, p, s, d, b, g in events:\n    pd_cnt[(pr, batches[b][1])] += 1\nvc2 = collections.Counter(pd_cnt.values())\nprint('events per producer-day:', dict(sorted(vc2.items())))\nEOF","description":"Test producer effects and batch clustering among events","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":243,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3682,"cache_read_input_tokens":193816,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3682,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":53,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"b560aaec-705e-4e98-b0e1-88ddc9fefd38","request_id":"req_011CbwRMafbmH9nrAVHmV9S6"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_012A8Csey8PZU3dzK15m51Ci","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01YBYTHhptrhVnyb6RQx4Wed","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport numpy as np, pickle, collections, pandas as pd\nfrom scipy import stats\n\ngrej = np.load('gamma_rej2.npy')\nSOLD = np.load('sold_panel.npy')\nkeys = pd.read_csv('panel_keys.csv').iloc[:,0].values\n\n# events per series (consumer-product) at gamma>0.8 vs exposure (purchase count)\nev_per_series = (grej > 0.8).sum(axis=1)\npurch_per_series = (SOLD > 0)[:, :364].sum(axis=1)\nlam = ev_per_series.sum() / purch_per_series.sum()\nprint('overall events:', ev_per_series.sum(), 'mean purchases/series:', purch_per_series.mean().round(0))\n# variance test: under independent-per-purchase, ev_i ~ Poisson(lam * n_i)\nexp = lam * purch_per_series\ndisp = ((ev_per_series - exp)**2 / np.maximum(exp, 1e-9)).sum() / (len(exp)-1)\nprint('dispersion index (1=Poisson):', round(disp, 3))\nvc = collections.Counter(ev_per_series.tolist())\nprint('events per series dist:', dict(sorted(vc.items())))\n# expected Poisson mix\nfrom scipy.stats import poisson\nexpc = collections.Counter()\nfor n in purch_per_series:\n    mu = lam*n\n    for k in range(8):\n        expc[k] += poisson.pmf(k, mu)\nprint('expected:', {k: round(v) for k,v in sorted(expc.items()) if k<8})\n\n# events per consumer (across products): sum over products for same store|cons\nsc = ['|'.join(k.split('|')[:2]) for k in keys]\nsc_ev = collections.defaultdict(int); sc_n = collections.defaultdict(int)\nfor i, k in enumerate(sc):\n    sc_ev[k] += ev_per_series[i]; sc_n[k] += purch_per_series[i]\nev = np.array(list(sc_ev.values())); nn = np.array([sc_n[k] for k in sc_ev])\nexp = lam*nn\ndisp2 = ((ev-exp)**2/np.maximum(exp,1e-9)).sum()/(len(ev)-1)\nprint('\\nper-consumer dispersion (across products):', round(disp2, 3), 'n consumers:', len(ev))\nprint('max events one consumer:', ev.max(), 'expected max ~', int(exp.mean()+4*np.sqrt(exp.mean())))\n\n# per store-product dispersion\nsp = [k.split('|')[0]+'|'+k.split('|')[2] for k in keys]\nsp_ev = collections.defaultdict(int); sp_n = collections.defaultdict(int)\nfor i, k in enumerate(sp):\n    sp_ev[k] += ev_per_series[i]; sp_n[k] += purch_per_series[i]\nev3 = np.array(list(sp_ev.values())); nn3 = np.array([sp_n[k] for k in sp_ev])\nexp3 = lam*nn3\ndisp3 = ((ev3-exp3)**2/np.maximum(exp3,1e-9)).sum()/(len(ev3)-1)\nprint('per store-product dispersion:', round(disp3,3))\nEOF","description":"Test consumer and store-product concentration of events","timeout":120000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5143,"cache_read_input_tokens":197498,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5143,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":44,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"dc00ade8-c048-4c58-bc50-e620d8ebe653","request_id":"req_011CbwRSqfsznnTT4ypu6ztp"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01JCwE9mqZEpZcE6qDwUETDk","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01785nMctM3UhZ4ViWt6rzbk","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport numpy as np, pickle, collections, pandas as pd\ngrej = np.load('gamma_rej2.npy')\nSOLD = np.load('sold_panel.npy')\nkeys = pd.read_csv('panel_keys.csv').iloc[:,0].values\n\nev = (grej > 0.8).sum(axis=1)\nn = (SOLD > 0)[:, :364].sum(axis=1)\ncells = collections.defaultdict(lambda: [0,0])\nfor i, k in enumerate(keys):\n    s, c, p = k.split('|')\n    cells[(s,p)][0] += ev[i]; cells[(s,p)][1] += n[i]\n\nrows = [(s, p, e, nn, e/nn) for (s,p),(e,nn) in cells.items()]\nrows.sort(key=lambda x: -x[4])\nprint('store-product cells by event rate (top 30):')\nfor s,p,e,nn,r in rows[:30]:\n    print(f'{s:>5} {p:>5} events={e:4d} purchases={nn:6d} rate={r:.4f}')\nprint('...bottom 10:')\nfor s,p,e,nn,r in rows[-10:]:\n    print(f'{s:>5} {p:>5} events={e:4d} purchases={nn:6d} rate={r:.4f}')\nrates = np.array([r[4] for r in rows])\nprint('\\ncell rate stats: min %.4f p25 %.4f p50 %.4f p75 %.4f p90 %.4f max %.4f' % tuple(np.percentile(rates,[0,25,50,75,90,100])))\n# histogram\nhist, edges = np.histogram(rates, bins=20)\nfor h, e1, e2 in zip(hist, edges[:-1], edges[1:]):\n    print(f'{e1:.4f}-{e2:.4f}: {\"#\"*h} {h}')\nEOF","description":"Map event rates across store-product cells","timeout":120000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4460,"cache_read_input_tokens":202641,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4460,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":66,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"93d9c5b4-3fb9-4d27-838b-e0ffbd0d690f","request_id":"req_011CbwRYFq5n3Zzjx6FjUy5P"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01NqkCjfaVLwExPUZhPaRSN5","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0125KgEBMqUF39TWpJyk5mr8","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport numpy as np, pickle, collections, pandas as pd\ngrej = np.load('gamma_rej2.npy')\npurch = pickle.load(open('purchases.pkl','rb'))\nD = pickle.load(open('deliveries.pkl','rb'))\nbatches = D['batches']\ndf = pd.read_pickle('sales.pkl')\nkeys = pd.read_csv('panel_keys.csv').iloc[:,0].values\nkey_ix = {k:i for i,k in enumerate(keys)}\nrows = (df.Store_ID + '|' + df.cons.astype(str) + '|' + df.Product_ID).map(key_ix).values\ndays = df.Day.values; solds = df.Sold_Quantity.values\nidxs = df.index.values\n\nfor (S, P) in [('S_7','P_24'), ('S_4','P_3'), ('S_2','P_10'), ('S_5','P_9')]:\n    sel = (df.Store_ID.values==S) & (df.Product_ID.values==P) & (solds>0) & (days<=364)\n    ii = np.where(sel)[0]\n    evs = []\n    pr_exposure = collections.Counter()\n    for i in ii:\n        segs = purch.get(idxs[i])\n        prs = {batches[b][0] for b,_ in segs}\n        g = grej[rows[i], days[i]-1]\n        for pr in prs: pr_exposure[pr] += 1/len(prs)\n        if g > 0.8:\n            evs.append((days[i], sorted(prs), [b for b,_ in segs], round(float(g),3)))\n    evs.sort()\n    print(f'\\n=== {S} {P}: {len(evs)} events ===')\n    pr_ev = collections.Counter()\n    for d, prs, bs, g in evs:\n        for pr in prs: pr_ev[pr] += 1/len(prs)\n    # producer event rate vs exposure within cell\n    tot_ev = sum(pr_ev.values()); tot_ex = sum(pr_exposure.values())\n    rows_out = []\n    for pr in pr_exposure:\n        e, x = pr_ev.get(pr,0), pr_exposure[pr]\n        rows_out.append((pr, e, x, e/x))\n    rows_out.sort(key=lambda r: -r[3])\n    for pr, e, x, r in rows_out[:8]:\n        exp_e = tot_ev*x/tot_ex\n        print(f'  {pr:>7} events={e:5.1f} exp={exp_e:5.1f} purchases={x:6.0f}')\n    # day clustering of events\n    ds = [d for d,_,_,_ in evs]\n    print('  event days:', ds[:40])\nEOF","description":"Drill into hottest store-product cells","timeout":120000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3817,"cache_read_input_tokens":207101,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3817,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":50,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"0d1bbdb0-78ad-4b8f-a36f-a0b360457a33","request_id":"req_011CbwRbCN47Eo7KYroictqR"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_0194z3WpZvDTfD2j9Y644FN3","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01M7BJ2cNncD6asVT98PSr1N","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport numpy as np, pickle, collections, pandas as pd\ngrej = np.load('gamma_rej2.npy')\npurch = pickle.load(open('purchases.pkl','rb'))\nD = pickle.load(open('deliveries.pkl','rb'))\nbatches = D['batches']\ndf = pd.read_pickle('sales.pkl')\nkeys = pd.read_csv('panel_keys.csv').iloc[:,0].values\nkey_ix = {k:i for i,k in enumerate(keys)}\nrows = (df.Store_ID + '|' + df.cons.astype(str) + '|' + df.Product_ID).map(key_ix).values\ndays = df.Day.values; solds = df.Sold_Quantity.values\nidxs = df.index.values\n\n# age = consumption day - batch producer-delivery day; one purchase may span ages -> use unit-weighted\nage_expo = collections.Counter()   # age -> unit-weighted purchases\nage_ev = collections.Counter()     # age -> gamma-weighted events\nfor i in range(len(df)):\n    if solds[i] == 0 or days[i] > 364: continue\n    g = grej[rows[i], days[i]-1]\n    segs = purch.get(idxs[i])\n    tot = sum(q for _,q in segs)\n    for bid, q in segs:\n        age = days[i] - batches[bid][1]\n        w = q/tot\n        age_expo[age] += w\n        if g > 0.8: age_ev[age] += w\n\nprint('age | exposure | events | rate')\nfor a in sorted(age_expo):\n    if age_expo[a] < 2000: continue\n    print(f'{a:>3} | {age_expo[a]:9.0f} | {age_ev.get(a,0):7.1f} | {age_ev.get(a,0)/age_expo[a]:.5f}')\nEOF","description":"Rejection rate as a function of unit age at consumption","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6899,"cache_read_input_tokens":210918,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":6899,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":26,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"8a7d710b-3812-4f46-a658-282ba6013969","request_id":"req_011CbwRhE8dw4ypGpwQTUgbr"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01XjDjSYpA5rAtUmE5mJh1QQ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01B9JmG3HrLzxzcYToE71xQL","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport numpy as np, pickle, collections, pandas as pd, json\n\ngrej = np.load('gamma_rej2.npy')\npurch = pickle.load(open('purchases.pkl','rb'))\nD = pickle.load(open('deliveries.pkl','rb'))\nbatches = D['batches']\ndf = pd.read_pickle('sales.pkl')\nkeys = pd.read_csv('panel_keys.csv').iloc[:,0].values\nkey_ix = {k:i for i,k in enumerate(keys)}\nrows = (df.Store_ID + '|' + df.cons.astype(str) + '|' + df.Product_ID).map(key_ix).values\ndays = df.Day.values; solds = df.Sold_Quantity.values; prods = df.Product_ID.values\nidxs = df.index.values\n\nNULL_FLOOR = 0.00103  # per-purchase E[gamma] under no-rejection sim\n\nstats = collections.defaultdict(lambda: [0.0, 0, 0, 0, 0.0])  # gsum_single, n_single, c90, c99, gmax\nfor i in range(len(df)):\n    if solds[i] == 0 or days[i] > 364: continue\n    segs = purch.get(idxs[i])\n    prs = {batches[b][0] for b,_ in segs}\n    if len(prs) != 1: continue\n    pr = next(iter(prs)); p = prods[i]\n    g = grej[rows[i], days[i]-1]\n    st = stats[(pr, p)]\n    st[0] += g; st[1] += 1\n    if g > 0.9: st[2] += 1\n    if g > 0.99: st[3] += 1\n    st[4] = max(st[4], g)\n\nres = []\nfor (pr,p), (gs, n, c90, c99, gmax) in stats.items():\n    ev1 = gs - NULL_FLOOR*n          # corrected evidence\n    ev2 = gs - 2*NULL_FLOOR*n        # conservative (2x floor)\n    p_ge1_cons = 1 - np.exp(-max(ev2, 0))\n    res.append((pr, p, gs, n, c90, c99, gmax, ev1, ev2, p_ge1_cons))\n\nprint('total supplying pairs with single-producer consumed purchases:', len(res))\narr = np.array([r[9] for r in res])\nfor bar in [0.5, 0.8, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99]:\n    print(f'pairs with conservative P(>=1 rejection) >= {bar}: {(arr>=bar).sum()}')\nc90s = np.array([r[4] for r in res])\nprint('pairs with >=1 single event gamma>0.9:', (c90s>=1).sum(), '; >=2:', (c90s>=2).sum())\ngmaxs = np.array([r[6] for r in res])\nprint('pairs with max gamma > 0.99:', (gmaxs>0.99).sum(), '> 0.95:', (gmaxs>0.95).sum())\n\n# weakest pairs detail\nres.sort(key=lambda r: r[9])\nprint('\\n20 weakest pairs:')\nprint(f\"{'pr':>7} {'prod':>5} {'gsum':>7} {'n':>5} {'c90':>3} {'c99':>3} {'gmax':>6} {'P>=1cons':>8}\")\nfor r in res[:20]:\n    print(f'{r[0]:>7} {r[1]:>5} {r[2]:>7.1f} {r[3]:>5} {r[4]:>3} {r[5]:>3} {r[6]:>6.3f} {r[9]:>8.3f}')\nwith open('final_pair_stats.pkl','wb') as f: pickle.dump(res, f)\nEOF","description":"Final per-pair evidence with conservative corrections","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2999,"cache_read_input_tokens":222371,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2999,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":61,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"6f6f2e4f-36e5-4945-ad30-697dff168325","request_id":"req_011CbwRsj4iD6tN4BvXYrTaM"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_014TGNVCwKFZCpKRznaVj4ji","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_015SwuuS7CyEWLrz41oryycq","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pickle, json\nP = pickle.load(open('parsed.pkl','rb'))\nsup = P['sup']\npairs = set()\nfor (day, pr), items in sup.items():\n    for p in items: pairs.add((pr, p))\nprint('distinct delivering (producer, product) pairs:', len(pairs))\nans = json.load(open('/app/answer.json'))\nans_pairs = {(pr, p) for p, lst in ans.items() for pr in lst}\nprint('answer pairs:', len(ans_pairs))\nprint('delivered but not in answer:', sorted(pairs - ans_pairs))\nprint('in answer but never delivered:', sorted(ans_pairs - pairs))\nprint('per-product counts:', {p: len(v) for p, v in list(ans.items())[:6]}, '...')\nEOF","description":"Cross-check answer against delivery matrix","timeout":60000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1957,"cache_read_input_tokens":227575,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1957,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":29,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"6b2f478a-fcdf-49ef-babf-300a1501d3be","request_id":"req_011CbwRwtcYefLkXxUaUb1Q8"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01Gfqc4JkpcEJ96V578X3mn9","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The answer is written to `/app/answer.json`: for each product, the confirmed set is **every producer that supplied that product** (728 producer–product pairs total, ~24–32 producers per product). Here's how the analysis got there and why I'm confident in that conclusion.\n\n## What I did\n\n**1. Exact supply-chain reconstruction (fully validated).** I parsed all four raw sources (4 file formats each), then simulated the wholesaler: producer deliveries enter per-product FIFO queues in daily acceptance-sequence order (1–60), stores are fulfilled in store-sequence order (1–10) with brutal first-come allocation, leftovers carrying over. This reproduced all 3,650 store receipt totals **exactly**. Then I simulated each store's FIFO batch queue against the sales log (consumers are identifiable as fixed-order blocks within each day's log) — all 2,741,217 rows satisfied `Sold = min(Demand, available inventory)` with **zero inconsistencies**, under the timing \"sales happen before same-day delivery arrival.\" So every unit each consumer bought is traced to a specific producer batch with certainty.\n\n**2. Rejection detection via a purchase-triggered HMM.** Demand is i.i.d. shopping (p≈0.48) × quantity ≈ round(base × product-calendar × lognormal noise, σ≈0.19). I found that demand=1 occurs at only 0.25% on days 1–2 (when recovery is impossible) but ramps to 4.4% over exactly 14 days — direct, model-free evidence of a dense purchase-triggered 14-day process. I built an HMM (states: normal / recovery day 1–14; transitions only at observed purchases; stage emissions pinned to the documented 20/50/80% multipliers, which match the unbiased event profiles). Forward–backward gives a calibrated posterior γ per purchase that it triggered a rejection. Calibration on synthetic null/injected data: γ-sum matches true counts; at γ>0.9 precision is ~98–99%.\n\n**3. The structural findings that determine the answer.** Rejections fire at ~0.9% of purchases (~14,700/year). I tested every axis for offender structure:\n- **No producer effect**: within-product chi-square is flat across all 30 products (1/30 at p<0.05 — pure chance).\n- **No batch clustering**: multi-rejection batches occur at or below chance — quality is effectively per-unit, not per-batch.\n- **No picky-consumer concentration** (dispersion 1.1), but **strong store-product structure** (dispersion 6.0; rates 0.07%–2%) — thresholds vary by store and product, identically across producers.\n- No meaningful age/staleness gradient.\n\n## Why the confirmed sets are \"all suppliers\"\n\nEvery supplying pair had ≥1,100 single-producer purchases consumed, so at the measured uniform rates every pair caused 10–25+ rejections during the year. Even using only direct evidence — the product of calibrated per-purchase posteriors, after subtracting a conservative 2× noise floor — 724/728 pairs exceed 95% probability of ≥1 attributable rejection (all 728 under the honest floor), 717 have at least one individually near-certain event (γ>0.9) with a single-producer trigger purchase, and 681 have two or more. The data decisively rules out a sparse offender structure; the only producers *not* confirmed for a product are the ones that never delivered it (each producer supplies only ~12–16 of the 30 products, so the sets are far from \"everyone\")."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":88,"cache_read_input_tokens":231087,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":88,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5cbbd6aa-1710-402d-a107-503834fd0351","uuid":"707ffce4-9d18-4987-a3f3-a3162c766c32","request_id":"req_011CbwRzdQapqkYy5jEb8Gdm"}
